title: PALOVUE Wireless Earbuds, in-Ear Earphones with Bluetooth 5.3, Built-in Mic, 35H Playtime Deep Bass Stereo, with Lightweight Compact Charging Case for Sport/Work Compatible iPhone Android
features: 【Reliable Bluetooth 5.3】PALOVUE wireless earbuds with cutting-edge Bluetooth 5.3 innovation provide instant and steady transmission, providing you with a continuous calling and music experience as well as noise cancellation.
【Automatic Pairing】When removed from the charging case, the wireless headphones would be moment-matched and auto-reconnect with the last-matched device.

【Ergonomic Lightweight Design】The ergonomic in-ear design of the headphones fits in each ear and comes with four different types of silicone ear tips to accommodate every ear profile for a pleasant wearing experience. They weigh 4g apiece and can be worn effectively without pressure, while the charging case weighs 28g and can easily fit in your pocket and be hefted around, making it ideal for commuting, working out, and travelling, among other things.

【Clear HD Sound】The Bluetooth codec supports AAC/SBC and is designed with premium speakers, providing you powerful highs and profound bass and allowing you to enjoy superb music. They include two built-in microphones for clear and raucous calls, making them suitable for any scenario, such as webinars and online classes.

【Waterproof Smart Control】Equipped with IPX5 waterproof grade protects the headphones from perspiration during workout. Ideal for cycling, jogging, exercise, skiing, touring, trekking, and other outdoor activities. Also enables dual-ear easy touch volume/music/call/voice assistant control, among other things, and can switch between single and binaural modes at will. Reliable product quality combined with powerful technology and 2-years after-sales service.

reviews.title: They bump!
reviews.text: These are amazing! For the price, they bump, and now canceling for sure! Nice small size not bulky case. Many sizes to fit many people included. I love them already! Functionality of tapping seems really good also. I was able to move them in my ear without interruption of accidental tap. Very pleased.

title: SIMOLIO Wireless Headphones for TV Watching with Transmitter/Charging Dock 2 in 1, 2.4G Wireless Earbuds for TV Listening, Dialog Mode & Boosted Vol & Spare Battery for Seniors, by-Pass, 100ft Range
features: WIRELESS EARPHONES FOR TV FOR HARD OF HEARING W/ TV AUDIO LISTENING ASSISTANCE - Voice clarity and noise reduction tech reduces distracting background noises and makes you hear each word spoken on your favorite show with full intelligibility. Automatic signal control tech stabilizes sudden increases in TV volume to prevent damage to your hearing. Supports optical, RCA and 3.5 mm audio outputs on most TVs. Not recommended for pacemaker carriers.

ADVANCED 2.4GHZ AUTO FHSS TECH, NO PAIRING OR DELAY, 100FT LONG TRANSMISSION - Operating on the 2.4GHz radio frequency, this tv wireless earphone has superior sound reproduction and a powerful signal that can pass through ceilings or walls. No pairing required, no lip-sync delay, no need to be facing a certain angle or position, and no interference when someone passes by.

PROFESSIONAL WIRELESS TV HEADPHONE, CUSTOMIZE THE TV SOUND TO BEST FIT YOU - people with different sound frequency sensitivities can select TONE (treble/normal/bass) to find the best parameters and hear the target source clearly. The BALANCE control is specifically designed for people with different hearing levels in the left and right ears. You can select STEREO on the transmitter to enjoy Hi-fi music or MONO to enjoy clearer TV conversations.

UNIQUE 3 IN 1 DESIGN TV HEADPHONES FOR FULL CONVENIENCE, ERGONOMIC NECKBAND & SOFT EARBUDS & SPARE BATTERY FOR LASTING TV ENJOYMENT - Transmitter, charging base and headset stand 3-in-1 design for your full convenience. The built-in rechargeable and replaceable Li-battery supports 7 hours playtime. A spare battery is always in the base charged & ready for lasting TV enjoyment. The ergonomic neckband design and ultra-soft ear tips bring you an easy and comfortable TV viewing experience.

WIRELESS EARBUDS FOR TV WITH INNOVATIVE “BY-PASS” FEATURE SUPPORTS SIMULTANEOUS AUDIO TRANSMISSION TO WIRELESS HEADPHONE AND WIRED TV SOUBNDBAR - If you only have one TV audio output and it is currently occupied by your soundbar or AV receiver, the by-pass feature can help you, and it’s the best solution for families watching TV together.

reviews.title: Nice headphone set
reviews.text: This is an excellent headphone set. I am hard of hearing and watch tv late at night, often the volume is too high and I disturb my family’s sleep, this set solves the problem, now I can hear the tv and they get a sound night’s sleep!. The ear pieces fit comfortably and great sound quality. I like that they are wireless and comfortable to wear.

reviews.title: Exactly as described, Quality product! Solid design!
reviews.text: Exactly as described, Quality product! Solid design!

title: FiiO Q5S High Resolution DAC and Headphone Amplifier
features: FiiO Q5S high resolution DAC and headphone amplifier

reviews.title: The little DAC with big magic
reviews.text:
This gadget is simply ahead of its time. For audio enthusiasts (like me) who dare to explore how the right DAC can elevate the experience of music listening to a delightful level, this DAC won't disappoint. It brings out the best in headphones (reasonably decent ones), which in my case were a Sennheiser HD 600 and a budget triple-driver IEM. Also, it breathed life into the premium speakers of my old car--simply unbelievable; I never heard them sound so good (the DAC's line-out was fed into the car's aux-in). My tracks came from Amazon HD Music.

As a gadget aficionado in general, I find this DAC well-designed, functionality- and aesthetics-wise, and with a solid build and classy finishing that clearly distinguish it from the other DACs in this price range. It's one of the best gadgets I bought during recent years.

reviews.title: No quality control.
reviews.text: Ordered a “like new” used item. Description said it had a 4.4mm headphone jack.  The box was a relatively new, but item inside was nothing like described.

reviews.title: Much better than expected...Updated!!! MQA use...
reviews.text:
02/16/21
I've been using the Q5s TC for some weeks now mainly with Apple Music and sometimes the SIRIUSXM app for iOS on my iPhone 12 Pro. Just a days ago I decided to check out MQA music on TIDAL. I have multiple headphones and settled on Shure SRH1540 and SRH1840. I have listed a number of headphones in other reviews so I won't go there. Anyway, WOW!!!, what a revelation. Listening to MQA's through TIDAL is very revealing and at one point on some recordings I had to say to myself, "there were anymore detail and layering I would be able to taste and smell it". I can also easily hear what I can assume is in some cases budgets, engineering and anything else that might affect the way a recording sounds.

Now from my understanding the Q5s does not unpack the MQA music file but it appears. it's definitely seeing and recognizing the file, if not totally able to unpack it. I say this because the indicator on the front glows a solid yellow which according to FiiO is AptX HD, don't know but it makes a difference. Talking about spacial clues? all over the place. But of course it depends on the recording. I can say this, listening to all my old new music, from Aretha to Soundgarden and more is so much fun and enjoyable.

I'm a ex-audiophile that actually love music more than the equipment (most of the time). The Apple AirPods Max reviews and rages made me revisit headphone collection. After selling 3 and decommissioning 1 I ended up with my mostly hated (not by me, I love the way sound) Sennheiser HD700. Measurements and other people's bias' aside I decided to put a dac/amp in my setup for my iPhone 12 Pro. The Q5s plugs right into my iPhone via the supplied connector with no issues at all. The bluetooth works great as well. I prefer direct connection over using BT mainly because I want to as close to the true signal as possible. AAC sounds great through this dac/amp combo. I can't hear a real difference the 2. The Q5s is a solid and professional quality dac/amp combo. It does get a little warm while running but nothing that concerns me. I'm also using the Shanling UP4 which is to my ears good too and serves another purpose in modest collection. The Shanling is not capable of plugging directly into the iPhone but does a really decent job with BT. I prefer the direct connection ability of the Q5s. Of course like I mention the build quality is in a different league as it should be due to the price difference between the 2.

reviews.title: Ain’t loud enough...
reviews.text: It sounded low volume, muffled & cluttered to me. Didn’t sound like an *amp* with dual chips. BT wasn’t strong enough either.

reviews.title: They fixed all of my complaints with the Q5
reviews.text:
1) One USB port
2) Volume knob is also the power-on, as found on the Q1
3) Nice input selector on the right with some labeled LEDs that also show battery charging level
4) Fewer buttons
5) Bluetooth 5.0 can do 24-bit/96khz, so need cable only for charging once Apple supports aptX HD (audiophile-only issue)

Before going any further, I upgraded the firmware from 0.04 to the latest (0.39). The frequency response with 0.39 seems a lot smoother.

The Q5s sounds different from Q5. It's a clearer, more detailed sound. I also like that the bass level is a little higher with the OFF setting. I use ATH-M50x headphones.

So, FiiO, if you are listening, here are my new suggestions:

a) Replace the microUSB port with USB-C. It's a much more reliable connection. microUSB gets loose over time.
b) Have a bass switch or knob that allows for more bass settings. Actually, the EQ feature on the FiiO app is probably a better way to go, assuming it is remembered when not using the app. I have not tried it yet. If that's the case, why not get rid of the base boost button altogether?
c) Have different sleep timeouts for charging vs. not charging. Add 1 hour, 2 hour, and Never as possibilities to each.

Non-FiiO problem: macOS and iOS do not support Bluetooth aptX HD or LDAC, so I will need to wait for 24-bit/96khz over BT 5.0. I submitted a bug to Apple. I hope others will do the same.

title: USB C Hub, Baseus 6-in-1 Portable USB C Adapter for iPad Pro, Upgrade Type C Dongle to 4K HDMI, USB 3.0, SD/Micro SD Card Reader, 3.5mm Earphone Jack, Type C PD Charger, Independent Switch
description:
It easily extends the USB-C port of your laptop to 4K HDMI, 2 USB 3.0 Port, TF Card Reader, fast USB-C PD charging port and 3.5mm Headphone Jack. Plug & Play, with no additional drivers or installation necessary.
Features:
Turns a single USB-C connection into 6 ports (3.5mm Audio Jack, 2 USB 3.0, Micro SD, USB-C, HDMI) Take full advantage of iPadOS with the hub that supports both USB-C and USB-A flash/hard storage drives. Connect to USB-A peripherals via high speed USB 3.1 Gen 1 capable of 5Gb/s transfer speed. Use hub and charge iPad Pro at the same time! Also supports USB-C data. Simply connect with the HDMI port, mirrors your laptop's display (supports 4K at 30Hz ) on any HDMI-enabled device. Dual USB 3.0 ports, transmission rate up to 5Gbps, can transfer a high-definition movie in seconds. 3.5mm Audio and Mic 2 in 1 Port makes listening, talking, or recording a breeze. Built in TF slot for easy access to files from universal Micro SD Memory Card(SDXC: with high storage capacity up to 2TB)
Specification:
Material: Aluminum alloy Colour: Deep gray Input:Dual Type-C  Output: USB3.0*2+HDMI*1+PD*1+3.5mm*1+TF*1 USB3.0 transmission speed: 5Gbps HDMI Resolution: 4K@30Hz  PD input power: 100W Max.  TF Card: Supports data retrieval from 2TB Memory Card  3.5mm Audio Frequency Port: Port supports standard US headphone sets Size: 83*36*19mm Weight: 65g
Package List:
1* USB C Hub Adapter 1* User Manual
features: Creative Mobile Hub - The Newest Version 6-in-1 USB-C Hub instantly turns your iPad Pro and laptop USB-C port to a 4K HDMI port, 2 USB 3.0 Ports, a USB C charging port with power delivery, 3.5mm Audio/mic 2-in-1 jack (CTIA) and a TF card reader. Plug & Play, with no additional drivers or installation necessary. (NOTE: Only iPad Pro of iOS 13 or above is supported.)

Fast Charge & Data Transfer - The USB-C PD charging port supports pass-through charging of up to 60W, quickly charges iPad Pro while using the other ports, and can connect USB C flash drive for data transfer. USB 3.0 supports transfer speeds up to 5Gbps, fast enough to transfer and share images, files, and videos in seconds.

Crystal Clear 4K Resolution - Mirror or extend your screen with the USB C adapter’s HDMI port and directly stream 4K 3840*2160 @ 30Hz or Full HD 1080p video to HDTV, monitors, or projectors for a cinema-like visual feast. (Tips: Video output for 2018 iPad Pro is 2K@60HZ resolution). And the 3.5mm headphones Jack ensures you talk and listen to music at the same time, keep your focus on call or music.

Pad/Laptop Matching Design - Align the L-shaped holder against the corner of your pad or other USB-C devices, Baseus USB C hub can be securely attached. It is recommended not to wear a protective cover or other accessories that affect the thickness of the device. (such as magic keyboard)

Universal Compatibility Compatible with new iPad Pro 2020/2019/2018, MacBook Pro, MacBook Air 2019/2018, MacBook, Google Chromebook, Dell XPS 13 15, Lenovo Yoga 900, HP Spectre notebook, Surface Book 2, Google Pixel 3 XL/3, Samsung Galaxy Note 10/Note 9/S9/S8/Note 8/, Huawei Mate 30/20/M10/P30/P20, and more USB-C laptop/smartphone.

reviews.title: Don’t buy this knock off, get the real deal
reviews.text: This is the knock-off brand. The real version of this has an SD & micro SD card slot. This version also stopped working for me in under a month. I first noticed it wouldn’t regularly connect to my monitor via the HDMI port, then it began failing to connect all together. The power pass through on this is awful, charging takes forever, but it does with apples dongles also so whatever. Get the real deal & save 50 bucks

reviews.title: I wanted to love this,
reviews.text: I got this for my new iPad. It’s supposed to let you use wired headphones, SIM cards, and even hdmi.  And it does.  It’s a bit cumbersome to use but it does sit on the corner of my iPad even with the iPad cover on.  The big issue i have with it is that it does get hot, VERY HOT, while you’re using it for a longer period of time.  I don’t use it often due to that but i got it at a good price so I’ll keep it.

reviews.title: So far it works as advertised!  Gets alarmingly HOT.. concerned about longevity..
reviews.text:
update::: I've been having issues with connecting this hub to a capture device. it will constantly disconnect and reconnect. I purchased an hdmi splitter that has it's own power source (micro usb) and I connect it with the ipad and the capture device. It's like a middle man... and now the this hub stops disconnecting and reconnecting. It's very odd. Also, connecting power via the USB C port, on the hub, does not help. It's also not a heat issue because I bought a mini fan to blow on it and it's never hot. However, it doesn't disconnect and reconnect when directly connected to a monitor/tv.. it works fine then.

in addition to that issue, I had to get a replacement and noticed that both hubs displayed different color profiles. Using the same monitor, cables, ipad, etc. One was sort of washed out and one was very saturated. Also one wouldn't display 4k (only 2k) and the other one would display 4k, 2k, 1080p, etc. Not really sure why there would be so much variance with the same product.. I guess QC issues?

-----------------------------------

This is the "UPGRADED" version.

So far it works as advertised! Gets alarmingly HOT.. concerned about longevity of the hub itself and concerned about potential damage this could do to my iPad...

This product advertises "Heat vents" but they're just cutouts for the ipad's speakers.. it doesn't actually vent the inside of the hub. This seems deceptive.

I've tried those $20-30 cheap usb c to hdmi + charging port options and they all didn't work properly... mostly they wouldn't actually charge the ipad while outputting the HDMI signal.
This device actually DOES charge while outputting the HDMI signal properly.. which is great.
The audio port works fine, but I have not used the USB ports as I don't really have any use for them at the moment. I mostly needed hdmi output and a charging port that actually charged while hdmi was being used.

I love how the company included 3 different sized spacers to accommodate for ipad's with cases.

The switch to disable the hub is a nice feature. BUT I wish you could disable the charging while still being able to use the HDMI out... so if this switch instead only disabled the charging capabilities but still retained the ability to output video/audio via the HDMI output, that would be, imo, better. Then when the ipad's own battery gets low.. I could flip the switch and allow the hub to start charging. Obviously I can just unplug the USB C charging cable from the hub, but it would be a QoL feature to not have to do that.

I like how it has a long-ish cable instead of just having the usb port extend out of the actual hub body.. this makes it much more versatile, imo.

My biggest concern is how long this product will last due to how HOT it gets while using the HDMI out and charging the ipad at the same time. This is the only time the hub gets alarmingly HOT.. when one is using the HDMI out AND charging at the same time. One could argue that because it's so hot to the touch, that means the passive cooling, inside the hub, is doing its job.. but I don't know if that's actually the case w/o seeing the internals and seeing what kind of heatsinks or w/e are being used. All I know is that this hub gets VERY VERY HOT and devices that overheat tend to stop working sooner than later.

Overall I think this is one of the better options available for 30-50$. The versatility of attaching to the ipad or have it hanging is a huge plus for me.

reviews.title: Convenient & Useful iPad adapter
reviews.text:
So far the only bummer is it’s not really screen protector friendly. I used the thinnest insert because without one it won’t stay in place.

It’s a bit too snug to put on an iPad 11 with a screen protector. The edge of the protector kinda bubbles up. I’ll have to try a few thin cases and not use the inserts to this adapter. That would be more beneficial anyways.

One thing I did notice about the charging port is it’s a bit loose. So you may need to get new cables or try different brands. I have several brands/styles and some do better than others. The Apple cables seem to fit fairly snug though.

Other than that it works as stated. I do like it and it’s more low profile compared to others. All of the ports work especially for charging multiple devices. I use 30w+ for my iPad because I tend to use adapters often. 30w brick & adapter loaded still charged iPad under use.

The adapter looks nice as well. A bit thick but not to the point it’s an issue using it. I have the model with the on/off switch. That’s convenient while otg, in case you need to cut power from the adapter to save the battery from draining.

The adapter doesn’t seem to take a ton of power but if you don’t need it on while away from a power source I’d say just flip it off.

Overall I like this adapter it’s nice & convenient. Like I said my biggest gripe was not really screen protector friendly (iPad w/out a case only screen protector) but it’s not a deal breaker. I’d recommend trying this out!

title: Defean 90mm / 3.54 inch Earpads Cushion Replacement Ear Pads for Akg k845 K545 K 845 545/JBL E50BT E50 BT S500 S700 Headphone/Pioneer HDJ1000 HDJ2000 HDJ1500 Headphones (White Ear Pads)
features: Condition: Brand New
Color: whtie
Material:Protein leather and memory foam
Compatible with: Akg k845 K545 K 845 545/JBL E50BT E50 BT S500 S700 Headphone/Pioneer HDJ1000 HDJ2000 HDJ1500 Headphones
You will receive: 1 pair of ear pads (2pcs )

reviews.title: Fits HDJ X5 but not very comfortable
reviews.text: It absolutely works, but the extra thickness does not provide extra support around ears. The foam is way too soft and it is being squished too much to be supportive and my ears are always touching the mesh inside the earpads. Plus, it is heating up more than the stock earpads.

title: Monolith M1060C Closed Back Planar Magnetic Over-Ear Headphones, Low Distortion And Perfectly Balanced Sound
description: The Monolith M1060C planar headphones are the perfect way to experience your music collection. The M1060C features a completely redesigned and engineered planar driver, producing a detailed aural landscape in a closed back enclosure. Spectacular imaging, low distortion, and perfectly balanced sound make the Monolith M1060C a true audiophile listening experience.
features: Planar Drivers: Not your typical headphone driver! The planar driver in the M1060C has been completely redesigned and engineered versus the driver found in the M1060.
Several enhancements have been made, including a new symmetrical magnet structure that allows the planar driver to be much more efficient in a closed back enclosure.

Closed Back Design: Outside noise stays out and only the music stays in! The 1060C delivers excellent audio isolation, allowing the sonic landscape to not be disturbed by the outside world, and the outside to not be disturbed by your music.
This makes the M1060C the perfect audiophile choice for those that may need to listen in less than silent environments.

Comfort: The Monolith M1060C has been engineered to be comfortable for extended listening sessions. Large, comfy earpads made of faux leather and a padded headband are designed to keep you comfortable as you listen.

reviews.title: These sound pretty great, if you get lucky
reviews.text:
I got the m1060c.
I got lucky, my pair was good. I didn't have 5k ring.
Frequency response is kinda flat though, so if you're into that, go for it. I found other, supposedly lesser, headphones more fun.
The base is deep, but not overwhelming .. and goes on for miles.
Mids are pretty good, though I like them being more forward.
Highs are clear and nice.
Construction is kinda meh, but solid enough to last a few years, but not sure beyond that.
The headband is kinda silly and cheapo.
Pads .. I found them exceptionally comfortable, but your mileage will vary.
Cable is nice though!
Quite hefty as a headphone.
They aren't exactly closed, or at least not very good at isolation.
Respond well to EQ, seem to have plenty of tunability.

Recommended if you like it flatish and want to try a planar.

reviews.title: I liked them
reviews.text:
They were decent with an amp, without they sounded a little thin. Weight was around 580 grams without the cord.
I opened the box and it looked like lots of spots inside, I thought it was a return and maybe someone sprayed something inside of it. Amazon sent me another pair but that box also had spots but less. I shined a dark light on it and this is what I observed. Maybe mildew, I don't know. 3 1/2 - 4 stars, could be a 5 star, they do feel very good and this is comparing them to the Mr Speaker Aeon Flow closed backs.

Pros
Really nice Bass
Sounded good for gaming very good imaging
Very comfortable deep pads

Cons
Maybe weight
Too bright for me

reviews.title: Great sounding Cans, though build quality is less than spectacular
reviews.text:
Love the wonderful sound of these cans however, some things to note before purchase:

1) Very Heavy (Probably the heaviest I've ever worn.
2) Cups swivel 360 degrees. This can tangle the wires easily if you are not careful.
3) Mine came with a defective cable with an intermittent right side connection. I've requested a replacement. Discovered that the only ones making a replacement for this is Monoprice .. Though they do offer an upgrade cable for around $35.00 that looks far more substantial.
4) Wood ear cups are stained very dark (walnut color). So much so that you cant really tell there is a beautiful zebra wood pattern. Would suggest that manufacturer offer non-stained version.
5) Metal frame is a little unwieldy (read large ) and somewhat flimsy. A new frame design should be considered.

All that said, they sound wonderful , so the other faults must be taken into consideration along with their reasonable price.

reviews.title: Great sound
reviews.text: The headband is an abomination. It hurt my head within 15 minutes of wearing.  The reason  I'm keeping it is because of how good it sounds. It's been the best closed backs I've tried, compared to some of the best rated ones like the dt177x go. I order a zmf pilot pad to fix the headband issue. All in all this headphone delivers a well detailed performance,  and if you can get past the comfort issue, you're in for a treat.

reviews.title: Versatile
reviews.text:
Yes. Versatile. These come from the factory as closed headphones and sound good in their stock form but where they really shine is if you convert them to open back headphones. You can google "1060C open mod" and you'll find a couple different companies selling the plates to mod these to open. The driver was designed for an open back headphone, so that is why they sound better when you open them up. There are ways to leave the wood cups on and turn them into a semi-open headphone as well and that will give you the benefit of improved SQ with a little less sound leakage but I think it's best to just switch between closed and open. Anyway, I've really enjoyed having the ability to switch from open or closed depending on the environment I'm in. Super useful. That said, I probably won't be taking these to the coffee shop with me, they're a bit too heavy for that.

As far as the headband is concerned, the other reviewers griping about it are correct, it sucks. However, I don't think it's bad as some of them have made out. Maybe it just works with my head shape but I've worn them for several hours at a time without any issues. I plan on getting the ZMF Pilot Pad ($30) and I've read that that basically solves any comfort issues with these.

The cable looks and feels just fine but beware that it is a little microphonic. I ended up buying a replacement for $17 on Amazon and that solved the problem.

All in all, I think these are great headphones. There are a couple of issues but they're solvable and once you solve them (ZMF Pilot Pad + new cable), they are outstanding in both their sound and comfort.

title: Maeline Bulk Earphones with 3.5 mm Headphone Plug - 30 Pack - Multi
features: Stereo Earbud style headphones with a 48-inch length cable and a 3.5mm audio jack plug with an L shape connector
Each earbud is individually wrapped with a twist tie and individually bagged in a ziplock bag
Fully compatible with Smartphone, iPhone, iPod, iPad, Cell Phone, Notebook, Laptop, Computer, Desktop, PC, Chromebook, MP3 player, Tablet and other devices with a 3.5mm audio plug (standard)

reviews.title: Some do not work
reviews.text:
I haven’t tried them all yet, but I’ve used four so far.
1. Didn’t work on either earpiece
2. Stopped working on one side and then stopped working on both sides
3. Same as #2
4. Only one side worked. Still using this one.

reviews.title: Only one pair worked(for two months )
reviews.text: They didnt  fit into my phone. (Loose)

reviews.title: These are junk dont waste your money.
reviews.text: These are junk. Do not purchase. Bought for my middle school class thinking I could seek them cheaply and I have trouble giving them away. Many are broken right of of the package. Absolutely waste of money.

reviews.title: Garbage do not purchase
reviews.text: Extremely cheaply made. The wires are super long and tangle within moments of use. I bought them for my son because he always loses them but he hasn't had a pair last long enough to lose. They're junk don't waste ur money.

reviews.title: Quantity over Quality
reviews.text: You can only hear sound from one side for some of the earbuds, and they're pretty fragile. Trying to untangle one of them ended up with me ripping off some of the plastic and exposing the wires accidentally. I also only received 91 earbuds instead of the promised 100. I would buy again though, just because it's so cheap.

title: MRPLUM Earbud Carrying Case Small Compatible with AirPods PU Leather Hard Portable Earphone Case Protective Storage Pouch Bag with Mesh Pocket & Keychain for Wireless Headphone USB Cable (Brown)
features: 【PREMIUM PU LEATHER & RETRO STYLE 】MRPLUM earphones case is made of top quality PU leather and hard EVA interlayer without any odor, durable, anti-fingerprint&light weight, exquisite workmanship and vintage outlooking can make you looks more stylish.

【COMPACT SIZE & MULTIPURPOSE】The earphone carrying case outer size is 2.8*2.2*0.8inch, it is easy to put it in your pocket, or bag, and the inner size is 2.6*2*0.8 inch, is perfectly compatible with Airpods 1/2/Pro, wire eaphones, data cables, keys, USB,SD card & change.

【ALL ROUND PROTECTION】Designed with premium zipper & material, the Ear Buds Case can well protect devices from scratching, dropping, featured with pressure-resistant and water-proof. keep the items in the case always clean and safe.

【MESH POCKET INSIDE】The Earbud Pouch has a mesh pocket inside, prevent your earbuds, or other items from moving, also avoid the wireed eaphone or the cord hang together, ensure make better use of space and hold all items in a neat condition.

【EASY TO CARRY & ANTI-LOST】The Earphones Case equipped with heavy duty metal keychain to attach it to your belt, backpack & bag easily, and carry it everywhere, it is also a practical design to avoid loosing your eaphones, keys and some important but mini objects.WARM TIPS: The package ONLY included a hard shell earbuds carrying case.

reviews.title: Nice little bud case
reviews.text: I could imagine a few other ways to utilize this little zippered case, designed for earbuds. But, it works very well for it's intended purpose. Even for some styles that have the 'wrap around' type support, as shown in my pics. It was a little snug, but they fit. The standard buds (either wired or wireless) fit much better. The case itself is nice 'pleather', good zipper and nicely appointed swivel snap to attach it to whatever you want to hang it on. My wife chose the natural leather color to go with one particular purse. She hangs it on the outside of the purse, like a 'bling' effect. Looks great. Good purchase. Five stars.

reviews.title: Just what the doctor ordered
reviews.text: I was looking for a case that would hold my slightly over-sized earbuds without it being too large or too bulky. These fit perfectly in my briefcase and there is plenty of room for my over-sized buds. It's a really  attractive case  for the money. I'm not sure if that is real leather, but who cares as long as they protect  what's inside. The added hook comes in handy since I can attach it to a loop in my briefcase so that it doesn't sink to the bottom and I have to dig for it.

reviews.title: Neckbands are fine, but earhooks are useless for me
reviews.text:
The neckbands worked fine, and all items gripped my gen 2 AirPods okay, but the earhooks were not even a little useful for me.

I’m used to wearing headphones with integral earhooks, and loved the fit of those, but these add-on earhooks for Airpods do not have the right amount of rigidity, nor is it easy enough to rotate and position them appropriately with respect to the AirPod stem so that the combined geometry of the two would ever align to my ear. Because of those two factors, they simultaneously made my AirPods look kind of stupid while at the same time actually making the fit/hold even worse; they seemed almost more likely to fall out with the hooks than without them.

reviews.title: Poor quality silicone that easily tears
reviews.text: I didn't know that silicone could actually tear like this stuff has when I removed them from my AirPods. I'm past the return window and these literally ripped after about a month and a half of use when I was trying to slide them off the AirPods. Look elsewhere!

reviews.title: They constantly fail
reviews.text:
The sound is good but they constantly fail to connect with my windows 10 laptop. When they do connect, it is usually just one of the earbuds and then it disconnects a short time later. I guess the battery life is okay but I've never been able to run them down as I get so frustrated with the connectivity issues.
The final straw was when I took them out for a jog for the fist time. One earbud failed and now will not power on. Is it due to moisture? I'll never know & I don't care. These are going in the trash.

reviews.title: Refurb- came coated with previous owner's ear wax - Yuck !  UPDATE !
reviews.text:
non hygenic refurb process disappoints- didn't try the super gross earbuds

UPDATE - I spent the extra $20 for a new set. these have issues. the earbuds turn on by themselves in the case and:
1.) hijack the phone connection
2.) use up all the battery, then charge up, and then run down the battery...so when you go to use the earbuds, the batteries are drained in earbuds and the case

other reviewers write of similar problem

skullcandy chat says they think the case needs to be replaced- I can send them in and wait a few weeks for their warranty repair. but it appears these issues are in the design

or return to amazon within the return period.

yes, that.

reviews.title: Nice sound
reviews.text: These have become my favorite earbuds love the voice activated commands

reviews.title: Stopped Charging
reviews.text: Stopped charging after a month. Return window just expired a few days ago. Now I have to deal with returning through Skullcandy

reviews.title: Comfort!
reviews.text: So the sound quality is good, battery life is good but for me the most important point is they are comfortable. I've been through a LOT of ear buds in search of something that I can wear for several hours without my ears starting to hurt and these are them! Charging case doesn't feel very substantial, but since I don't need to charge them every couple of hours I can live with it.

reviews.title: Interesting app
reviews.text: I've been using these earbuds for several weeks now, and they're as good as any pair I have. And I have several. They connect without issue and have good, clear sound. If you're looking for the intricasies of fine tuning, how good is the base and all that, I'm not the gal for you. However, I think music sounds great, and they're perfect for listening to YouTube vids on my computer and audio books on my phone. The app is interesting. At first I thought I could talk directly to my buds. "Hey, Skullcandy!" (First earbuds I've had with voice control.) But you have to use the app for that feature. Not quite as convenient in my opinion, although perhaps I was expecting too much. The controls are not touch, but require clicking on the individual buds to control them. You can also control them through the app, though. Instructions are clear and easy to understand. I was up and running in minutes. All in all, I think they are excellent and definitely worth the price tag.

reviews.title: Big Lag
reviews.text: I use this headset for working from home, and every phone call is a struggle due to lag. I am constantly talking over people or being talked over by them. It's functional, but not ideal perhaps for phone calls.

reviews.title: Big Upgrade for me!!
reviews.text: These are fantastic!  I had a pair that pooped out in less than a year, I got these with my fingers crossed and so far they are a BIG UPGRADE!!! Voice clarity & noise canceling are great!  I work from home with 2 big dogs, it matters for me.

reviews.title: Very happy
reviews.text: I really like this headset.  I love the charging station as I often woukd forget to charge my last headset that was charged with usb cable.  My only issue with the headset is that the headband is not adjustable and I have a smaller head so the support bar sits a little low.  Very good connection and stays connected if I move around the house.

reviews.title: It Won't Charge
reviews.text: It worked wonderfully for a couple months and now it won't charge. I have switched outlets thinking it was my outlet. I also make sureits lined up in charging base. I loved it until it stopped working. Its a little pricey to only get 2 month use.

reviews.title: Comfortable for truckers and the office.
reviews.text:
I am a dispatcher and I have to wear a headset 8hrs a day. I can stand this one for about 6 hrs. before I need to switch ears. That is better than most headsets I have used. My office orders $300 headsets and this one can throw down with the best of them. The noise cancelation is excellent and the calls sound loud and clear. The Bluetooth connection last the entire 8+ hour shift. Once paired it connects seamlessly when I set up for my day.

Before you spend hundreds of dollars on a headset, give this one a chance. I am glad I did.

I hope this helps. :-)

reviews.title: Great earbuds that fit well, sound good, and have long battery life!
reviews.text: The earbuds have pretty good sound and the main reason I love them is most earbuds don't fit well in my ears, but the ear fins make it snug and a good fit. They are fairly easy to connect to bluetooth devices. The case seems to hold a lot of battery and lasts a long time and I like that it charges with USB-C. The ear fins on the earbuds make it very snug and a good fit in my ears, and this also makes it noise cancelling. The case is a nice small size too. I've used it for almost a month and I have no complaints about them :)

reviews.title: VERY IMPRESSED!

NO EAR FATIGUE.
These foam earphone tips are softer than silicone tip material, Great elasticity and recovery make the tips perfectly fit your ear canal, very comfortable.
BETTER COMFORT
Slow-rebound material, uniquely breath-activated earmuffs activated by body temperature, gives you a new experience without irritation or fatigue.
PREMIUM SOUND.
Soft foam earbud tips provide a perfect seal in the ear canal and pure audio with enhanced bass while eliminate the outside noise.
MORE ISOLATION
With the ear canal properly sealed by the earbud tips, you don't need to increase volume to enjoy music in a noisy environment.
LITTLE USING TIPS
Before wearing earbuds, squeeze the sponge tips with your hands, so they will automatically inflate and perfectly fit your ear canal after wearing. That will create better sound insulation. The memory foam is about 11mm high, the largest diameter is about 12mm, and the inner diameter of the catheter is about 3mm.Made of high-quality, environmentally friendly and non-toxic materials, it has the advantages of softer, slower rebound and more durable. Memory foam is characterized by a slow rebound effect, which can change its own shape with external pressure. From the perspective of earplugs, the ear canal varies from person to person, and the memory foam will change their shape according to the individual's ear canal, making them fit the ear canal best, thus achieving more than ordinary eartips cover good wearing comfort, better noise reduction effect, bring better listening experience. reviews.title: Not a quality product
reviews.text: I needed these to fit my Sony earbuds which are always smaller than normal earbuds. I've used Comply in the past and loved them but they're expensive. Should have bought them anyway. These are small plastic tubes inserted into blue memory foam. The tubes don't stretch to wrap around the tip of the earbud the way quality products do and the tubes are apparently not glued to the memory foam as they separate when you're trying to install them.

reviews.title: Hold really well
reviews.text: Really like these foam tips. They hold extremely well in my ears whether I'm hitting hitting the punching bag, jogging or cutting grass.

reviews.title: The quanity you get for the price + the quality is excellent
reviews.text: I like the comfort, quality, and price of these replacement earbuds.  The foam holds up well to use, they never fall off, and they do block out sound around you but not so much you can't hear that someone spoke to you.  The price and quantity is the best on Amazon

reviews.title: Not so great
reviews.text: I bought these as an inexpensive replacement for my ear buds. They took quite a bit of twisting and pushing to get into my ear without feeling like they would fall out. With that, after just a couple days, the outer section broke away from the inner so they no longer fit on the ear bud. I do not recommend purchasing.

reviews.title: Perfect replacement size
reviews.text: Very comfortable, noise isolating, fits my older Phillips SHR 9850 earbuds like new. Like the color because you can tell when they are dirty & need to be replaced.

reviews.title: Cute and functional
reviews.text:
Positives
1. The headphones are easy to adjust over your ears. Just a little stretch and readjust. You can wear it forwards or backwards.
2. Easy bluetooth connection. Connects easily and quickly when I turn it on.
3. Sound quality is okay.
4. A nice thickness that keeps you warm while listening to music
Negatives
1. The knot in the front has a thick seam which puts pressure on my forehead. It's uncomfortable the longer I wear it.
2. Medium to full volume level, other people can hear.
3. Micro USB
Nice for a warm headband but not great for my misophonia.
